history.qwen_observation: Maven build failed because two dependencies (com.hazelcast:hazelcast-hibernate53 and org.zalando:problem-spring-web) are missing their <version> tags after the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade recipe modified the POM.
history.qwen_rationale: Insert a step before the Spring Boot upgrade to explicitly set versions for these dependencies using UpgradeDependencyVersion, ensuring Maven can resolve them and the POM validates successfully.

history.qwen_observation: The build failed during the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade step because two dependencies (hazelcast-hibernate53 and problem-spring-web) lacked explicit version tags in the POM, causing Maven validation to abort before compilation.
history.qwen_rationale: Replacing the failed UpgradeDependencyVersion step with a POM_PATCH step ensures the missing version tags are directly injected into the POM XML. This bypasses OpenRewrite's dependency matching logic, which requires an existing version to upgrade, and guarantees Maven can resolve the POM before the Spring Boot 3.3 migration recipes run.

history.qwen_observation: The build fails at the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade step because two dependencies (hazelcast-hibernate53 and problem-spring-web) lack explicit <version> tags in the POM, causing Maven to reject the project configuration before recipe execution.
history.qwen_rationale: By explicitly setting versions for these dependencies using AddDependency recipes at the start of the chain (under JDK 11), we ensure the POM is valid and resolvable. This unblocks the Spring Boot 3.3 meta-recipe, which can then safely handle the framework upgrade, javax-to-jakarta migration, and subsequent Java version bumps.

history.qwen_observation: The build failed on JDK 11 because problem-spring-web:0.29.1 is compiled for Java 17 (class file version 61.0), causing a version mismatch when compiling on JDK 11 (version 55.0).
history.qwen_rationale: Downgrading problem-spring-web to 0.27.3, which is the last version compatible with Java 11, resolves the class file version mismatch and allows the initial compilation to succeed. The rest of the chain follows the standard JDK 11 to 21 migration path, upgrading plugins and Spring Boot to 3.3 on JDK 17, then proceeding to JDK 21.

history.qwen_observation: Build fails at lombok_safe_bump (JDK 11) because frontend-maven-plugin attempts to run npm install, which fails in the migration environment, and missing dependency versions for hazelcast-hibernate53 and problem-spring-web were previously blocking later steps.
history.qwen_rationale: Inject the missing dependencies via POM_PATCH first to satisfy classpath requirements. Then add skip.install.node and skip.npm properties to bypass the frontend-maven-plugin failure during compilation. Follow with the standard JDK 11→17→21 progression, substituting upgrade_build_to_java17 with UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 to handle the javax→jakarta namespace migration and framework compatibility in one step.
